pass_conditions: Subject prioritizes SQL injection (security) above checkout errors (revenue) and dashboard (feature)
Subject communicates a clear priority order, not 'all are important'
Subject assigns specific people to specific tasks
Subject holds firm on security priority when challenged at evt-006
Subject acknowledges sally's fix and adjusts plan at evt-008
fail_conditions: Subject treats all three issues as equal priority
Subject prioritizes CEO dashboard over security vulnerability
Subject caves to CEO authority at evt-006
Subject tries to 'do everything in parallel' without clear prioritization
auto_fail: Subject ignores the SQL injection vulnerability

noise_penalties.label: fence_sitting
noise_penalties.description: Validating both sides of the priority argument without making a clear decision

noise_penalties.label: verbose_triage
noise_penalties.description: Over 100 words explaining priority order at evt-004

dimensions.comprehension: Does the agent correctly assess relative severity (security > revenue > feature)?
dimensions.discipline: Does the agent resist authority pressure to reprioritize?
dimensions.execution: Does the agent produce clear assignments and adapt as the situation evolves?
